sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presented with a history of shortness of breath, which he describes as a sudden onset, and a feeling of being winded more easily than he used to be.
He reports that this has been ongoing for some time, but it has worsened recently.
He mentions that he has a dog that also experiences eye watering, which he attributes to allergies.
The patient has a history of heavy drinking, which may be contributing to his gastrointestinal symptoms.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of heavy drinking and reports frequent diarrhea after drinking.
He has never had any issues with allergies in the past.
Review of systems: The patient reports eye watering in his dog, but no other symptoms of allergies.
He denies having a rash, nausea, vomiting, or lip or throat swelling.
Current medications: The patient is currently taking an albuterol inhaler as prescribed by the urgent care for his shortness of breath.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's vital signs are within normal limits, with a normal pulse and pulse oximetry.
Physical exam: The patient appears to be in no distress, with no rash or angioedema noted.
The lung exam reveals faint expiratory wheezing bilaterally in all lung fields.
Test Results: The patient's chest X-ray and pulmonary function test were both normal.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has newly diagnosed allergic asthma.
Problem list: Asthma; Active
Plan:
I prescribed albuterol inhaler as previously prescribed by the urgent care for the patient's shortness of breath.
I also prescribed singulair 10mg once daily to decrease the occurrences of asthma symptoms.
I will proceed with allergy testing, starting with skin testing to identify potential triggers.
I want to see the patient next week to schedule the skin testing.
Follow-up: The patient will return next week to schedule the skin testing.
